key_themes: Semiconductor/AI boom: 18-day chip-stock rally, Intel earnings ignite sector, Nvidia retakes $5T market cap
Iran/geopolitical risk: ceasefire extension and peace-talk hopes ease tensions, but ServiceNow flagged deal delays
Nuclear energy resurgence: X-Energy Amazon-backed IPO raises $1B+ as AI data center demand drives power needs
Cable cord-cutting crisis deepens: Charter loses 120K broadband subscribers, cable names hit hard
GLP-1 weight-loss drug expansion: Hims & Hers adds Eli Lilly Zepbound/Mounjaro to platform

stocks.reason: Q1 2026 earnings massive beat: EPS $0.29 vs $0.01 expected, revenue $13.58B vs $12.42B; data center revenue +22% to $5.1B; Q2 guidance well above consensus; stock surged ~24%, surpassing its 2000 dot-com peak for first time
Surged ~14% on Intel earnings sympathy rally; DA Davidson upgraded to Buy with $375 PT (from $220); Stifel raised PT to $320; renewed AI trade confidence lifted semiconductor peers
Closed +4.3% at $208.27, reclaiming $5 trillion market cap (first company ever to close above that threshold); record closing high aided by Intel earnings and AI infrastructure demand
Q1 2026 earnings miss: EPS $9.17 vs $9.97 expected; lost 120K broadband subscribers vs expectations; stock fell ~21%, one of biggest single-day declines in its history

Q1 beat on EPS ($0.97 vs $0.96) and revenue ($3.77B vs $3.74B) but stock fell ~14-18% — worst single-day drop ever — as Iran war caused ~75bp headwind from delayed large on-premise deals in the Middle East; Armis acquisition also pressured margins

IPO debut: Amazon-backed small modular nuclear reactor developer raised $1.02B (upsized, priced at $23, above $16-$19 range); opened at $30.11, closed +27%; largest nuclear public offering on record driven by AI data center power demand

Stock jumped +7% after expanding Eli Lilly GLP-1 partnership: platform now offers Zepbound, Mounjaro, and Foundayo via LillyDirect, alongside existing Novo Nordisk Ozempic/Wegovy access; broadens weight-loss drug offerings ahead of FDA compounding review

Fell ~3.7% as Hims & Hers partnership (routing GLP-1 prescriptions through LillyDirect) raised margin concerns; investors await full earnings call April 30 for clarity on deal economics and compounding competitive impact
Q1 2026 earnings beat; shares climbed ~3% in premarket and held gains; consumer staples outperformed defensively amid mixed broader market open

Fell ~8% after lowering 2026 profit guidance to low end of prior forecast; attributed shortfall to storm-related disruptions and lost revenue from Hurricanes Helene and Milton; unusual late-season hurricane impact on hospital volumes
Tumbled ~8% after Deutsche Bank downgraded to Hold from Buy, cutting PT to $34 from $35; Charter's massive subscriber-loss report amplified sector-wide cable concern

Q1 2026 earnings and revenue miss: EPS $6.44 vs $6.69 expected, revenue $18.02B vs $18.19B; free cash flow turned deeply negative (-$291M vs +$955M a year ago); F-16 and C-130 production delays flagged; stock fell ~4-5%
Downgraded to Neutral from Buy by MoffettNathanson alongside Flutter Entertainment (FLUT); online sports betting sector faces near-term headwinds cited by analyst
Wedbush initiated with Outperform and $225 PT, calling Oracle "on a path to become foundational infrastructure provider for the AI Revolution"; cloud and database momentum in focus
Morgan Stanley raised to Overweight from Equal Weight, lifting PT to $174 from $147; refining and midstream sector seen as beneficiary of easing Iran tensions and stabilizing oil prices
